symbols: bishop's vestments — Augustine's authority as Bishop of Hippo
book — his extensive theological writings including Confessions and City of God
description: A portrait of Saint Augustine wearing bishop's vestments, likely holding a book or crozier. The painting is in rich Venetian tones characteristic of Titian's religious works.
theological_description: Augustine of Hippo (354-430) was the most influential theologian of the Western Church. His conversion, catalyzed by reading Romans 13:13-14, transformed him from a dissolute intellectual into the greatest Christian thinker of the early centuries. His Confessions, City of God, and treatises on grace shaped Western theology profoundly. Augustine's key insight — that the human heart is restless until it rests in God — echoes the Psalmist: 'As a deer pants for flowing streams, so pants my soul for you, O God' (PSA.42.1).

scripture_refs.reason: The soul thirsting for God, Augustine's central theme
The truth will set you free — Augustine's intellectual journey
